title: Midea device quirks
description: Per-device fixes for cap-derived state that the protocol can't express on its own. Quirks describe a device's actual behaviour (Linux kernel-style 'quirks') and are applied after process_b5 + finalize_capabilities. Two operations: feature_available overrides and synthesized B5 capability records.

name.description: Short human-readable name shown in apply summaries.

description.description: Longer free-form explanation of why this quirk exists and what it fixes.

feature_available.description: Direct overrides for status['fields'][name]['feature_available']. Applied after finalize_capabilities so they override cap-derived decisions.

synthesize_capabilities.description: Synthetic B5 TLV records to inject into status.capabilities_raw. The existing resolve_capability_encoding() picks them up automatically. Useful for forcing an encoding selection when the device under-reports its capability tier.

cap_id.description: Capability ID as hex string, e.g. '0x16'.

data.description: Raw data bytes for the capability.

force.description: If true, replace any existing real cap with this cap_id. Default false: real device data wins, synthesis is skipped.
